plain_language.what_this_is: Hard, automatic caps that protect your Claude usage without you having to watch anything. max_turn_usd caps ONE turn's cost (passed to Claude Code's own --max-budget-usd flag, which cleanly stops a turn the instant its cost crosses this number). max_turns caps how many agentic turns (Claude working with its tools back and forth) a SINGLE message can fan out into (passed to Claude Code's own --max-turns flag) -- this stops one message from quietly running for hundreds of hidden steps and burning your plan, the 2026-07-20 failure mode. max_chat_usd caps a WHOLE chat's running total across many turns -- when it's crossed, PCC automatically gives that chat a fresh worker session (keeping all its visible history) so the underlying context can't keep growing unnoticed for hours. All are separate from your overall 5-hour/weekly usage limit shown in the app's usage meter.
plain_language.how_to_update: Raise max_turn_usd or max_turns if a normal heavy turn (lots of tool calls, a big build) is hitting a cap and getting cut off before finishing. Raise max_chat_usd if you want longer chats before an automatic rollover. Lower any for tighter protection. This file being missing, deleted, or broken NEVER disables any cap -- PCC falls back to safe defaults (3 dollars / 30 turns / 15 dollars) rather than running with no limit.
